lede: A look at February 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jigme Namgyel Wangchuck and Tomáš Kopecký, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Donald Trump
events.desc: United States President Donald Trump is acquitted by the United States Senate in his first impeachment trial.

events.title: National Revolutionary Front for the Liberation and Reconstruction of Haiti
events.desc: Rebels from the Revolutionary Artibonite Resistance Front capture the city of Gonaïves, starting the 2004 Haiti rebellion.

events.title: Apollo 14
events.desc: Astronauts land on the Moon in the Apollo 14 mission.

events.title: Cultural Revolution
events.desc: Cultural Revolution: The Shanghai People's Commune is formally proclaimed, with Yao Wenyuan and Zhang Chunqiao being appointed as its leaders.

events.title: United States Congress
events.desc: The Congress of the United States passes the Immigration Act of 1917 over President Woodrow Wilson's veto.

events.title: Claudio Monteverdi
events.desc: Claudio Monteverdi's last opera L'incoronazione di Poppea was performed theatrically for the first time in more than 250 years.

events.title: Hellenic Air Force
events.desc: Greek military aviators Michael Moutoussis and Aristeidis Moraitinis perform the first naval air mission in history, with a Farman MF.7 hydroplane.

events.title: Leo Baekeland
events.desc: Belgian chemist Leo Baekeland announces the creation of Bakelite, the world's first synthetic plastic.

events.title: Charles XIV John
events.desc: Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte ascends to the thrones of Sweden and Norway.

events.title: AD 62
events.desc: An earthquake with an estimated intensity between IX or X on the Mercalli scale occurs in Pompeii, Italy.

deaths.text: Irv Gotti, American record producer, co-founded Murder Inc Records
Tom McGuigan, New Zealand soldier and politician, 23rd New Zealand Minister of Health
Margaret Oakley Dayhoff, American chemist and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: Roger Williams, Anne Hutchinson (Episcopal Church (United States))
Christian feast day: 26 Martyrs of Japan (in Evangelical Lutheran Church in America and Anglican Church in Japan)
